title: Conscious Cystometry Bladder Function Testing

Life Sci 69:1193-1202, 2001","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bdt33","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"615C46CA341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":75,"is_image":true,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"1e28m\",\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":[]}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/jpeg","original_name":"uropathy.JPG","placeholder":"","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bib64kcn.jpg","width":66}}}}
steps: Use scissors to cut the sealed tube located between the animal’s ears.
Attach the end of the tube not connected to the metabolic cage swivel device to the tube between the animal’s ears.
Start the saline syringed filled pump (Kent Scientific Corporation, Torrington, CT, USA) to flush debris within the animal’s tube for about 2 minutes.

Using the preset pump configurations (3 ml/hour) fill the bladder via a stopcock with room temperature saline and record bladder pressure by using a pressure transducer connected to an amplifier (BP-100, CB Sciences, USA) for a period of 2-5 hours.
Collect urine in a beaker mounted on a force transducer (FT-03 D, Grass Instrument Co, Quincy Massachusetts) placed beneath each cage.
The pressure and force transducers should be connected to a multi-port controller with software (Polyview, Grass Instrument Co, Quincy Massachusetts) to record data through the computer.
creator: Lili Liang
